meta.title: Classic Lasagne
meta.originalText:
# Classic Lasagne

A proper layered lasagne with slow-cooked meat sauce and silky béchamel. Serves 6.

## Ingredients

### Meat Sauce
- 1 large onion, finely diced
- 2 medium carrots, finely diced
- 2 stalks celery, finely diced
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 500 g beef mince (80/20)
- 400 g crushed tomatoes (one 400 g tin)
- 70 g tomato paste (double concentrate)
- 120 ml red wine (dry Italian)
- 15 ml olive oil
- 5 g salt
- 2 g black pepper
- 3 g dried oregano

### Béchamel
- 60 g butter
- 60 g plain flour
- 750 ml whole milk
- 1 g nutmeg (freshly grated)

### Assembly
- 250 g dried lasagne sheets (no-boil)
- 200 g mozzarella, coarsely grated
- 50 g Parmesan, finely grated

## Equipment
- Large heavy-bottomed pan or Dutch oven
- Medium saucepan
- Whisk
- 9×13 inch baking dish
- Oven
- Cutting board and knife
- Grater

## Instructions

1. **Prep the soffritto**: Finely dice the onion, carrots, and celery. Mince the garlic.
2. **Build the base**: Heat olive oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add the diced onion, carrot, and celery. Cook for 8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until softened. Add garlic and cook 1 minute more.
3. **Brown the meat**: Increase heat to medium-high. Add the beef mince, breaking it up. Cook 6-7 minutes until well browned. Season with salt and pepper.
4. **Deglaze**: Pour in red wine and scrape up browned bits. Let bubble 2 minutes.
5. **Build the sauce**: Stir in crushed tomatoes, tomato paste, and oregano. Bring to a gentle simmer.
6. **Simmer**: Reduce heat to low, cover partially, and simmer for 25 minutes, stirring occasionally.
7. **Make the béchamel**: While the meat sauce simmers, melt butter in a medium saucepan over medium-low heat. Add flour and whisk constantly for 2-3 minutes until pale golden. Gradually add milk, whisking constantly to prevent lumps. Cook until thickened enough to coat the back of a spoon. Season with nutmeg and a pinch of salt.
8. **Preheat oven** to 180°C (350°F).
9. **Grate cheeses**: Coarsely grate the mozzarella. Finely grate the Parmesan.
10. **Assemble**: Spread a thin layer of meat sauce in the baking dish. Layer lasagne sheets, meat sauce, béchamel, and mozzarella. Repeat for 3-4 layers. Finish with béchamel and remaining mozzarella on top.
11. **Bake**: Cover with foil and bake 25 minutes. Remove foil and bake 10 more minutes until golden and bubbling.
12. **Rest**: Let stand 10-15 minutes before cutting — this is essential for clean slices.
13. **Serve**: Cut into portions and top with freshly grated Parmesan.

## Notes

The béchamel must be smooth — whisk constantly when adding milk. Resting after baking is essential; the layers set as they cool slightly. Leftovers reheat beautifully at 160°C for 20 minutes covered with foil.
meta.notes: The béchamel must be smooth — whisk constantly when adding milk. Resting after baking is essential for clean slicing. Leftovers reheat beautifully at 160°C for 20 minutes covered with foil.

operations.details: Heat olive oil in pan. Add diced onion, carrot, and celery. Cook 8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until softened and lightly golden.
Add minced garlic and cook 1 minute until fragrant
Add beef mince, break up with a wooden spoon. Cook until well browned with no pink remaining. Season with 5 g salt and pepper.
Pour in red wine, scrape up browned bits from the bottom. Let bubble until mostly evaporated.
Stir in crushed tomatoes, tomato paste, and oregano. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Cover partially and simmer for 25 minutes, stirring occasionally. Sauce should thicken and flavours meld.
Melt butter in the saucepan over medium-low heat
Add flour and whisk constantly for 2-3 minutes until the roux is pale golden and smells nutty
Gradually add milk in splashes, whisking constantly to prevent lumps. Cook until thickened enough to coat the back of a spoon. Season with nutmeg and 2 g salt.

Spread a thin layer of meat sauce on the bottom of the baking dish. Alternate layers of lasagne sheets, meat sauce, béchamel, and mozzarella. Repeat for 3-4 layers. Finish with béchamel and remaining mozzarella on top.
Place dish in oven. Bake 25 minutes covered with foil, then remove foil and bake 10 more minutes until golden and bubbling.

finishSteps.details: Remove from oven and let stand 10-15 minutes — essential for the layers to set and allow clean slicing.
Cut into portions and top with freshly grated Parmesan. Serve immediately.
